1→--- 2→description: View progress and associations for an objective issue 3→context: fork 4→agent: general-purpose 5→--- 6→ 7→# /local:objective-view 8→ 9→Displays progress and associations for an objective issue, including roadmap status analysis, associated PRs, and associated plans. 10→ 11→## Usage 12→ 13→```bash 14→/local:objective-view [<objective_number>] 15→``` 16→ 17→--- 18→ 19→## Agent Instructions 20→ 21→### Step 1: Resolve Objective Reference 22→ 23→Parse `$ARGUMENTS` for the objective number. 24→ 25→**If `$ARGUMENTS` is provided (non-empty):** Use the provided value directly as the objective number (parse as number or URL). 26→ 27→**If `$ARGUMENTS` is empty/not provided:** Infer the objective from the current branch: 28→ 29→```bash 30→erk exec resolve-objective-ref
... [214 lines omitted] ...
36→- If `"resolved": false`, display error: 39→Error: No objective number provided and could not infer from current branch. 60→Error: Issue #<number> is not an erk-objective issue (missing erk-objective label). 234→## Error Handling 236→- **Objective not found:** Display "Error: Objective #<number> not found." 237→- **GitHub API rate limited:** Display "Error: GitHub API rate limited. Try again later."

1→"""Shared workflow for preparing plans for worktree creation. 2→ 3→This module provides the canonical logic for preparing plans for worktree creation, 4→including validation, branch naming, and metadata extraction. Used by both 5→`erk wt create --from-plan` and `erk implement`. 6→""" 7→ 8→from dataclasses import dataclass 9→from datetime import datetime 10→ 11→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.schemas import BRANCH_NAME 12→from erk_shared.naming import ( 13→ InvalidWorktreeName, 14→ sanitize_worktree_name, 15→ validate_worktree_name, 16→) 17→from erk_shared.plan_store.types import Plan, PlanState 18→ 19→ 20→@dataclass(frozen=True) 21→class PlanBranchSetup: 22→ """Result of successfully preparing a plan for worktree creation. 23→ 24→ Attributes: 25→ branch_name: Git branch name (e.g., plnd/fix-bug-01-15-1430) 26→ worktree_name: Sanitized directory name for the worktree 27→ plan_content: Plan body to use as plan.md content 28→ pr_number: Plan number 29→ issue_url: Full GitHub issue URL 30→ issue_title: Issue title for reference
... [104 lines omitted] ...
32→ warnings: List of warning messages (e.g., non-OPEN plan) 42→ warnings: tuple[str, ...] = () 46→class PlanValidationFailed: 50→ message: User-facing error message explaining the failure 57→PreparePlanResult = PlanBranchSetup | PlanValidationFailed 74→ warn_non_open: Whether to include warning for non-OPEN plans 77→ PlanBranchSetup on success, PlanValidationFailed on validation failure 81→ return PlanValidationFailed( 88→ return PlanValidationFailed( 94→ # Collect warnings 95→ warnings: list[str] = [] 97→ warnings.append(f"Plan #{plan.pr_identifier} is {plan.state.value}. Proceeding anyway...") 102→ return PlanValidationFailed( 114→ return PlanValidationFailed( 115→ f"Generated worktree name failed validation.\n{wt_validation.format_message()}" 127→ warnings=tuple(warnings),

1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- 3→ Regenerate: erk-dev gen-exec-reference-docs 4→ CI check: erk-dev gen-exec-reference-docs --check (included in make fast-ci) 5→ 6→ Source of truth: Live Click command tree in src/erk/cli/commands/exec/group.py 7→ Generator code: packages/erk-dev/src/erk_dev/exec_reference/generate.py 8→ Generator docs: docs/learned/cli/auto-generated-reference-docs.md 9→ 10→ Regenerate after: adding/modifying/removing exec commands or changing help text 11→--> 12→ 13→# erk exec Commands Reference 14→ 15→Quick reference for all `erk exec` subcommands. 16→ 17→## Summary 18→ 19→| Command | Description | 20→| --------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | 21→| `add-objective-node` | Add a new node to an objective's roadmap. | 22→| `add-plan-label` | Add a label to a plan via the appropriate backend. | 23→| `add-plan-labels` | Batch add labels to multiple plans from JSON stdin. | 24→| `add-pr-labels` | Add labels to a PR with automatic retry on transient failures. | 25→| `add-remote-execution-note` | Add remote execution tracking note to PR body. | 26→| `capture-session-info` | Capture Claude Code session info for CI workflows. | 27→| `ci-fetch-summaries` | Fetch CI failure summaries for a PR. | 28→| `ci-generate-summaries` | Generate CI failure summaries using Haiku. | 29→| `ci-update-pr-body` | Update PR body with AI-generated summary and footer. | 30→| `ci-verify-autofix` | Run full CI verification after autofix push. |
... [1401 lines omitted] ...
105→| `summarize-impl-failure` | Summarize an implementation failure using Haiku. | 168→Add labels to a PR with automatic retry on transient failures. 212→Fetch CI failure summaries for a PR. 224→Generate CI failure summaries using Haiku. 434→| `--job-status` | CHOICE | Yes | Sentinel.UNSET | Job status (success or failure) | 1275→### summarize-impl-failure 1277→Summarize an implementation failure using Haiku. 1279→**Usage:** `erk exec summarize-impl-failure`

1→--- 2→description: Extract insights from plan-associated sessions 3→argument-hint: "[pr-number]" 4→--- 5→ 6→# /erk:learn 7→ 8→Create a documentation plan from Claude Code sessions associated with a plan implementation. The verb "learn" means: analyze what happened, extract insights, and create an actionable plan to document those learnings. 9→ 10→## Usage 11→ 12→``` 13→/erk:learn # Infers plan from current branch 14→/erk:learn 4655 # Explicit PR number 15→``` 16→ 17→## Purpose 18→ 19→All documentation produced by this command follows the content quality standards in the `learned-docs` skill. Load that skill for the full rules on audience (AI agents), the cornerstone principle (cross-cutting insight), and what belongs in learned docs vs code comments. 20→ 21→## Agent Instructions 22→ 23→**Prerequisite:** Load the `learned-docs` skill for content quality standards before proceeding. 24→ 25→Tell the user (choose the appropriate pipeline display based on whether `.erk/impl-context/` exists): 26→ 27→**When `.erk/impl-context/` exists (CI/async mode):** 28→ 29→``` 30→Learn pipeline for PR #<pr-number> (using preprocessed materials):
... [801 lines omitted] ...
55→Parse the JSON output and check the `labels` array. If `erk-learn` is present, stop with this error: 58→Error: PR #<pr-number> is a learn plan (has erk-learn label). 143→This returns JSON with plan details (`pr_number`, `title`, `state`, `url`, `head_ref_name`, `base_ref_name`, `objective_id`, `backend`) or an error if not found. Save the PR number for the parallel agents below. 413→Confirm you see the expected files (session-\*.md, diff-analysis.md, existing-docs-check.md, pr-comments-analysis.md) before proceeding. If any files are missing, the agent failed to write its output and must be re-launched. 544→If agents were not launched or failed, fall back to manual analysis. 573→- Error messages and how they were resolved 742→Parse the JSON output. If `count` is 0, no comment was added (no candidates found by the extractor). This is normal and not an error. 758→**If plan validation failed (no actionable documentation):** 824→- `<tool_result>` elements with errors often reveal the most useful insights

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: | `get-plans-for-objective` | Fetch erk-plans linked to an objective. |

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
### get-plans-for-objective
Fetch erk-plans linked to an objective.
**Usage:** `erk exec get-plans-for-objective` <objective_number>

### get-prs-for-objective
Fetch erk-plans linked to an objective.
**Usage:** `erk exec get-prs-for-objective` <objective_number>

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
### Step 3: Get Session Information
Run the exec script to get session details:
```bash erk exec get-learn-sessions <pr-number> ```

### Step 3: Get Session Information
Run the exec script to get session details:
```bash erk exec get-learn-sessions <pr-number> --learn-pr ```

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
### Step 5: Fetch Associated Plans
```bash erk exec get-plans-for-objective <objective_number> ```
Parse JSON output to get plans linked to this objective. The output has format:

### Step 5: Fetch Associated Plans
```bash erk exec get-prs-for-objective <objective_number> ```
Parse JSON output to get plans linked to this objective. The output has format:
